author: Ameen Hassan
summary: The user asked what needs to be done about 'the vault' next. The assistant searched local memory files and the AgentOS memory system for any existing context on 'vault setup' but found no prior documentation. A grep across the DHS Consulting directory found a single reference in Workshop_1_Files/Workshop/Welcome.md — a line reading 'When you're ready, delete this note and make the vault your own,' which is standard Obsidian onboarding text. The Workshop subdirectory contains a small set of files including dated notes and a copy of the BB_W1_Presenter_Guide. No other vault definition, scope document, or prior session capture was found. The session ended without resolving what 'the vault' refers to beyond identifying it is likely an Obsidian vault in the Workshop_1_Files directory.
problems: No prior memory or documentation exists for 'the vault' in any searched location

It is unclear whether 'the vault' is a separate project or part of the DHS Consulting/Workshop work

The only vault reference found is standard Obsidian onboarding text in Workshop_1_Files/Workshop/Welcome.md
Workshop subdirectory contains: 2026-05-07.md, BB_W1_Presenter_Guide.md, Untitled.md, Welcome.md
The vault appears to be an Obsidian vault located at Workshop_1_Files/Workshop/
open_questions: What is 'the vault' — is it the Obsidian vault at Workshop_1_Files/Workshop/ or something else?
What does the user want to do with or build in the vault?
Is the vault setup a separate project with its own scope and deliverables?
Was the vault setup discussed in a prior session that was not captured in memory?
